highlights: Alpine coaster rides through forested slopes with control over speed via handlebar brakes
Multiple tiered waterfalls with scenic bridges, wooden walkways, and viewing platforms
Combo packages available: zipline (1.5km), alpine coaster, and waterfall access

thingsToKnow.crowds: Peak crowds during midday and weekends; visit before 9am or after 2pm for shorter queues. Larger coaster gets very busy.
thingsToKnow.difficulty: Alpine coaster suitable for elderly. Some walking paths slippery during rainy season requiring extra caution. Walking alternative available to one waterfall.
thingsToKnow.safety: Weight restrictions: 120kg for zipline, 125-150kg for alpine carts. Staff may not always communicate safety requirements clearly. Coaster slower in rain for safety.
thingsToKnow.accessibility: Clean bathrooms on site. Well-maintained paths with steps and lush greenery. Easy parking for scooters (3,000 VND). Some staff speak English.
thingsToKnow.seasonal: Visit February-March after rainy season for powerful waterfall views. Avoid dry season if wanting to see waterfalls at full capacity. Still operates in light rain.

visitorTips: Book tickets online in advance for hassle-free entry. Alpine Coaster 3 is newer with longer track and better scenery.
Arrive around 3pm or early morning to avoid peak crowds and long queues at coasters.
Purchase combo packages (zipline + coaster + entry) for better value than separate tickets; round-trip coaster included.

faq.question: How much does it cost and what's included?
faq.answer: Alpine coaster alone: 240,000-250,000 VND per person including return trip. Combo packages: 1,000,050-1,000,000 VND for zipline (1.5km) plus coaster plus waterfall access. Entry to view waterfalls requires coaster ticket.

faq.question: What are the weight restrictions?
faq.answer: Zipline: maximum 120kg. Alpine carts: 125-150kg limit. Combined weight may require separate carts. Weight restrictions not always clearly communicated by staff.

faq.question: Can I visit all three waterfalls?
faq.answer: Waterfall 3 accessed via coaster. Waterfalls 1 and 2 accessible by hiking uphill or walking (some paths shown on maps are closed). Separate tickets required to access different waterfall areas.

sentiment.positive: Visitors consistently praise the alpine coaster experience, scenic beauty of waterfalls, helpful staff, cleanliness, and value of combo packages. Great for families and adventure seekers.
sentiment.negative: Long queues during peak times, confusing signage for which coaster to use, some staff communication issues with non-Vietnamese speakers, walking paths between waterfalls sometimes closed, smoking allowed in park, and requiring separate tickets to access all waterfalls.
